mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration, reducing aggregate consumer spending expectations and driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of instances where the unemployment rate exceeds the 7.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, directly increasing corporate revenues and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in real consumption and housing starts yields negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and slowing output, triggering immediate negative market repricing over short horizons.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines produce positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of tightening regime observations.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer price inflation and stagnant employment growth creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and forces negative equity returns over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed zero in more than 50 percent of months where the stagflationary trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with improved corporate earnings trajectories, generating consistently positive forward equity returns across market cycles.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over any 5-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 45 percent of high-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in total nonfarm payrolls below zero signals an acute labor market contraction, prompting immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 70 percent of months where payroll growth turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real consumption indicates inventory accumulation without demand absorption, leading to negative equity returns as firms cut prices and margins.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of episodes where industrial output rises while consumption falls.
